business.headline: Snabbit raises $56 million as instant home services race heats upSnabbit has raised $56 million to expand its instant home services business across India. The round underlines growing investor confidence in organising a vast but still informal market.

Got this HR email on salary reimbursements? It could help you save more taxIf you have recently received an email from your office's HR department on salary reimbursements, it may be worth a closer look. It could influence how part of your salary is taxed.

Crude oil at $110: Why it is a wake-up call for India and the worldCrude oil has surged towards $110 a barrel as markets react to war-linked supply fears in West Asia. The rise matters for India and beyond because it can feed into fuel, inflation, imports and household costs.

Up nearly 40% in 2 days: What's fuelling this pharma stock's rallyCohance Lifesciences shares surged after the company named Umang Vohra as Executive Chairman and Group CEO. The move lifted expectations of faster growth, stronger execution and bigger scale in its CDMO business.

Six brokerages, six buy ratings: The story that market is missing about PaytmTarget prices set by Bernstein, Goldman Sachs, Jefferies, Bank of America, Investec and Emkay Global range from 1,250 to 1,500, pointing to an upside of as much as 31% from its Monday close of 1,138.
8th Pay Commission website down as key Delhi meetings set to beginThe 8th Pay Commission website appeared inaccessible as consultations with employee unions were set to begin in Delhi.

8th Pay Commission: Will govt employee salaries rise as key talks begin today?The 8th Pay Commission will begin a three-day consultation with employee unions in New Delhi. The talks will shape decisions on pay, allowances, pensions and the closely watched fitment factor.

Sensex, Nifty open lower as rising crude oil prices hamper investor sentimentThe BSE Sensex was trading at 77,294.03, down 9.60 points or 0.01% at around 9:41 am. The NSE Nifty 50 stood at 24,127.90, up 35.20 points or 0.15%.

Nomura sees India's growth slowing early in FY27 before a later reboundNomura said India's growth could slow in the first half of FY27 before improving later in the year. The report said steady demand and policy support may aid recovery, though oil and global risks remain.

8th Pay Commission: From arrears to 3.83 fitment, what postal staff wantThe proposal has been submitted by the Federation of National Postal Organisations (FNPO) ahead of the Commission's consultation meetings scheduled in New Delhi from April 28 to 30.

Fertiliser prices surge globally amid Hormuz crisis. Will farmers be impacted?Currently, India has about 19.02 million tonnes of fertilisers in stock, compared to a requirement of 39.05 million tonnes for the kharif season.

UP rolls out 10-year 'warranty' for rural water schemesUttar Pradesh has made a 10-year maintenance period mandatory for rural drinking water projects under Jal Arpan. The shift aims to keep village schemes running longer through accountability, quality checks and lower power costs.

Oil India discovers oil, gas in Libya amid global energy tensionsThe state-owned firm informed stock exchanges that the discovery was made in the Area 95/96 block in Libya, located in the Ghadames Basin in the southwestern region of the country. The area is considered highly prospective for hydrocarbons.

Reddit user asks where to invest Rs 42 lakh. We got experts to answerA Reddit user recently asked a question many retirees quietly worry about, i.e., what to do with Rs 42 lakh after retirement. Should it stay safe in fixed deposits or be moved into mutual funds for better growth? Let's have a look.

India signs FTA with New Zealand: What it means for trade, jobs and investmentAt its core, the agreement focusses on making trade easier and cheaper. One of the biggest highlights is that New Zealand will remove tariffs on all 8,284 product categories. This means Indian goods will get duty-free access right from the start.

More people are stopping SIPs: Should you also consider exiting?More investors are pressing pause on their SIPs as markets turn volatile. Seeing losses can make anyone rethink their strategy, but is stopping really the right move, or could it hurt your long-term plans?

8th Pay Commission: 4 big demands including Rs 72,000 minimum pay and OPS returnAs talks around the 8th Pay Commission move forward, government employees are pushing for big changes, including a Rs 72,000 minimum pay and OPS return. But will these demands make the final cut? Let's have a look.

Sensex, Nifty close higher despite oil above $100, IT and pharma lead recoveryThe BSE Sensex closed at 77,303.63, up 639.42 points or 0.83%, while the NSE Nifty 50 settled at 24,092.70, gaining 194.75 points or 0.81%.

Infosys exits top 10 as weak outlook deepens doubts over India IT growthWith its market capitalisation now at around Rs 4.9 lakh crore, Infosys has dropped out of the top 10 list, while LIC has moved in with a valuation of about Rs 5.1 lakh crore.

MobiKwik shares jump 15% today: Why the stock is rallying sharplyMobiKwik shares surged after the RBI approved the group's NBFC licence application. The clearance sharpens its lending push and has raised expectations around the company's next phase of growth.

entertainment.headline: Bigg Boss Telugu fame Ashu Reddy accused of Rs 9.35-crore marriage fraud Hyderabad police have registered a cheating case against actor Ashu Reddy over allegations linked to a marriage promise and Rs 9.35 crore. Ashu Reddy has denied wrongdoing, while investigators said the enquiry is continuing.
Bobby Deol's Bandar postponed to June, avoids May box office clash Bobby Deol's Bandar has been pushed to June. The new date keeps it clear of two May releases and places it a day after Yash's Toxic.

Rashmika Mandanna returns as presenter at Anime Awards 2026, joins The Weeknd Rashmika Mandanna will return as a presenter at the 10th Global Anime Awards in Tokyo on May 23, where The Weeknd will also join the line-up. Her return follows her 2024 milestone as the first Indian presenter at the ceremony.

Aneet Padda's viral Shakti Shalini glimpse sparks buzz over her new avatar Aneet Padda's viral Shakti Shalini set video shows her in character as a Rajasthani schoolgirl. The glimpse has stirred curiosity about her looks and the film's place in Maddock's horror comedy universe.

Lisa Kudrow says Friends male writers discussed sexual fantasies about female cast Lisa Kudrow said the mostly male writers on the Friends set made harsh remarks about the cast and discussed sexual fantasies about her co-stars.

How Satinder Sartaaj recorded Dhurandhar 2's viral Jaiye Sajana hook in 15 minutes Satinder Sartaaj and Bhushan Kumar recalled how Jaiye Sajana for Dhurandhar: The Revenge was created in a last-minute rush. The song's organic response has led T-Series to release an extended version.

Michael box office: MJ biopic nears $220 million globally and Rs 20 crore in India Antoine Fuqua's Michael has earned $218.83 million worldwide in four days and is nearing the Rs 20 crore mark in India. The strong start comes despite mixed reviews, criticism over omitted allegations and a steady fall in daily collections.

Vijay's Jana Nayagan likely to hit theatres on May 8 after delays: Report Vijay's Jana Nayagan is likely to reach theatres worldwide on May 8 after repeated delays. The reported date has reassured fans after censor hurdles and an online leak clouded the release.

Shakira's Brazil show hit by tragedy after crew member dies on site A crew member died in an accident while assembling stage structures for Shakira's upcoming Brazil concert. Organisers and emergency officials extended support to the family.

Aamir Khan confirms 3 Idiots sequel, says script set 10 years later Aamir Khan has confirmed that Rajkumar Hirani is working on 3 Idiots 2, which picks up 10 years later. The sequel brings back Aamir's popular Phunsukh Wangdu and promises to revive the original film's humour.

Dhurandhar 2's 40 days at box office, and chances of beating Baahubali 2 this week Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar: The Revenge has entered its sixth week while closing in on Baahubali 2's worldwide total. The film needs about Rs 9 crore more to become the second highest-grossing Indian film worldwide.

KKK 15: Gaurav Khanna, Shagun Sharma and ex-contestants Rubina, Jasmin confirmed Khatron Ke Khiladi 15 has locked Gaurav Khanna and Shagun Sharma, while several former contestants are set to return. The mix of new faces and familiar names adds a twist as the show prepares for its comeback on TV and JioHotstar.

Nawazuddin Siddiqui slams actors using paid negative PR: This phase is at extreme Nawazuddin Siddiqui has spoken about actors using paid negative PR against one another in Bollywood. He said the trend has reached an extreme phase and is harming creativity.

Swollen face, broken tooth: Splitsvilla X6's Preet alleges assault, seeks justice Splitsvilla X6 contestant Preet Singh alleged that a man assaulted her at a Mumbai club and posted a video of her injuries on Instagram. In her post, she also noted an alleged involvement of a fellow female influencer.

CCTV operator on poll duty detained for watching leaked Jana Nayagan, sharing clips A CCTV operator, who was on election duty at Chennai's Anna University on April 23, has been detained for watching leaked clips of Vijay's Jana Nayagan. He shared photos of himself watching the illegal version of the film.

The Big Bang Theory spinoff Stuart Fails To Save The Universe: Here's what we know HBO Max has unveiled Stuart Fails to Save the Universe, a new Big Bang Theory spin-off led by Kevin Sussman. The series pushes the franchise into multiverse comedy as familiar faces return ahead of its July 2026 debut.

Actor-TVK chief Vijay visits Tiruchendur Murugan temple ahead of election results Actor and TVK chief Vijay visited the Tiruchendur Murugan temple in Tamil Nadu days before the elections results. His midnight arrival in Madurai drew heavy crowds, keeping attention on his public appearance, election results and Jana Nayagan.

Diljit Dosanjh returns to Jimmy Fallon's show as guest, turns it into Bhangra party Diljit Dosanjh has teased his return to The Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon with studio clips of him teaching the host Bhangra steps. The posts have built anticipation around the episode, though the nature of his appearance remains unconfirmed.

Verity trailer: Dakota Johnson uncovers Anne Hathaway's dark secrets The teaser trailer for Verity shows Anne Hathaway warning Dakota Johnson as a mystery unfolds around the Crawford home. The first look sets up a dark triangle of secrecy, desire and unsettling revelations.

general news.headline: Bengal Phase 2 looms with over 12 lakh SIR appeals pending, only 1,468 voters clearedWith Phase 2 of Bengal polls a day away, over 12 lakh voter appeals remain pending before tribunals. Only 1,468 have been cleared and six rejected, leaving lakhs uncertain about their voting status as deadlines pass and queues grow outside appellate centres in Kolkata.

Who is 'Singham' Ajay Sharma? Encounter specialist stirs storm in BengalThe entry of 'encounter specialist' Ajay Pal Sharma, an IPS officer from UP, in Bengal has created a flutter. A video of him warning supporters of a TMC candidate has gone viral. Here's the story of Ajay Sharma, who began his career as a dentist before turning into a tough and no-nonsense police officer.

More people are stopping SIPs: Should you also consider exiting?More investors are pressing pause on their SIPs as markets turn volatile. Seeing losses can make anyone rethink their strategy, but is stopping really the right move, or could it hurt your long-term plans?

Oil, LPG tankers reaching Mumbai despite US blockade of Hormuz. Here's howOil and LPG tankers and vessels have reached Mumbai from the Gulf despite the US blockade in the Strait of Hormuz. Experts say routes through Iranian waters and naval coordination limit the blockade's reach. We analyse whether the speculated routes are actually feasible.

Aadhaar is now linked to your Google account in India. Sort ofGoogle has joined hands with UIDAI to bring Aadhaar to Google Wallet. What this effectively means is that now not only you can link your Google account with your Aadhaar account but can also use Wallet to authenticate yourself in places where Aadhaar is demanded.

ChatGPT palm reader meme sparks fear CIA may have your fingerprintsDid you ask ChatGPT to do your palm or face analysis? If you have, the internet says the CIA now has access to your biometric details like fingerprints and face scans. It is an outlandish claim, of course. But it reflects a real anxiety about privacy and AI tools like ChatGPT.

Man who knifed guards near Mumbai was self-radicalised, NIA to probe: CM FadnavisMaharashtra CM Devendra Fadnavis said the Mira Road stabbing accused was self-radicalised through online content and literature, with a jihadist motive behind the attack on two security guards. The ATS and NIA are now probing the case, examining the accused's digital footprint and possible extremist links.

India fast-tracks urea factory plans in Russia amid Iran war-sparked crisisIndia's Rs 20,000-crore joint venture with Russia to build a urea factory with a manufacturing capacity of two million tonnes in Russia is expected to be ready within two years. The project, backed by three Indian firms and Russia's Uralchem, has been fast-tracked amid the fertiliser crisis due to the Iran war.

Forests across Uttarakhand are burning: The reason is not just dry weather2026 has added to the problem by ending an already dry winter early and instead bringing rising heat to the state, marking the arrival of what could be another worrying chapter.

Live: BJP wins 11 of 15 municipal corporations, Congress draws blankGujarat Local Body Election Result 2026 Live Updates: Counting for more than 9,000 local body seats will begin shortly in Gujarat after polls were held across 15 municipal corporations, 84 municipalities, 34 district panchayats, and 260 taluka panchayats on April 26 and 27. The polling witnessed a strong participation of the enthusiastic electorate, with 66% people voting across the state, braving the scorching summer heat. Key names included Union Home Minister Amit Shah, Chief Minister Bhupendra Patel, and Deputy Chief Minister Harsh Sanghavi. Catch all the live updates at indiatoday.in
Mumbai guard attack accused was US returnee, watched IS videos, taught mathsJaib Ansari, a 31-year-old US returnee, stabbed two security guards in Mumbai after questioning them about their religion.

Nashik hospital staff injured after head trapped in lift, accident caught on CCTVThe victim leaned into a small freight lift used to transport surgical equipment when it suddenly moved, trapping her head and causing critical injuries.
Scindia's onion tip during heatwave draws questions from doctorsA viral heatwave "hack" meets hard science- and reveals why surviving extreme summer is no place for shortcuts.

CCTV operator on poll duty detained for watching leaked Jana Nayagan, sharing clipsA CCTV operator, who was on election duty at Chennai's Anna University on April 23, has been detained for watching leaked clips of Vijay's Jana Nayagan. He shared photos of himself watching the illegal version of the film.

Got this HR email on salary reimbursements? It could help you save more taxIf you have recently received an email from your office's HR department on salary reimbursements, it may be worth a closer look. It could influence how part of your salary is taxed.

Biometric SIM checks to bank debit holds: Govt's plan to tackle digital arrestsThe report, prepared after consultations with stakeholders, seeks court directions to various authorities to ensure coordinated action against the growing cyber fraud threat. With this, the Centre is pushing for tighter accountability across telecom, banking and digital platforms to curb the surge in digital arrest scams.

Little boy cries inconsolably, smashes cricket bat after failing to meet Virat KohliThe little boy had been waiting at the reception of the hotel, hoping to get his cricket bat signed by Virat Kohli. As the RCB star arrived with his security and staff, the boy followed him and tried to approach, but was stopped by security personnel.

Cops raid Dehradun bar operating beyond hours, find senior IPS officer presentA late-night drive to shut a Dehradun pub was disrupted after IG Rajiv Swaroop was found at the spot. Uttarakhand Police has sought reports and is checking CCTV footage amid questions over coordination.

India mocked him, but his ghost number discovery runs the global code todayDattatreya Ramchandra Kaprekar's work on the number 6174 emerged from a small classroom in Devlali. The discovery later gained global attention even as he remained largely overlooked in India.

Crude oil at $110: Why it is a wake-up call for India and the worldCrude oil has surged towards $110 a barrel as markets react to war-linked supply fears in West Asia. The rise matters for India and beyond because it can feed into fuel, inflation, imports and household costs.

Urban India's new reality? Pay Rs 600 to talk, walk and hang out with a 'friend'Want someone to play badminton with, go grocery shopping, or spend time on PlayStation? Even accompany you to the hospital for an MRI? You can now pay for companionship.

Why travel ban sought on Muhammad Yunus amid 250 measles death in BangladeshA deadly measles outbreak in Bangladesh has killed over 250 people, mostly children, since March, the worst toll in two decades. A Supreme Court petition seeks to ban former interim chief adviser Muhammad Yunus from travelling, alleging his regime's push to privatise vaccination caused the crisis.

PM Modi bends it like Beckham in Sikkim but message is for BengalThe timing of PM Modi's political play is hard to ignore. In Sikkim's neighbouring state, Bengal, where the second phase of polls is due tomorrow, football isn't just a game - it's part of the state's cultural identity.

Terror hubs no longer immune to action: Rajnath Singh slams Pakistan at SCO meetAt the SCO Defence Ministers' meeting in Kyrgyz capital Bishkek, Rajnath Singh said countries sheltering terror infrastructure cannot expect immunity. He coupled the warning with a call for collective action, dialogue and regional stability.

sports.headline: Kohli says speaking in school is awkward, but gives golden message to students Virat Kohli delivered a golden message to young cricketers in Delhi as he inaugurated a branch of his childhood coach's academy on Tuesday, April 28.

Even in IPL rush, Virat Kohli makes time for childhood coach, inaugurates his academy Virat Kohli stayed back in Delhi to inaugurate his childhood coach Rajkumar Sharma's new academy branch at DPS RK Puram on Tuesday, highlighting their long-standing bond. He will travel to Ahmedabad for Royal Challengers Bengaluru's next match against Gujarat Titans on Thursday.

Manchester United close to Champions League spot, but still work to do: Carrick Manchester United interim manager Michael Carrick remains grounded despite his side edging closer to securing a spot in next year's Champions League. United need just two more points to secure the spot after their 2-1 win over Brentford.

Mind your business: Kris Srikkanth grills Hemang Badani over Fleming-Dhoni jibes IPL 2026, DC vs RCB: Kris Srikkanth tore into Hemang Badani after Delhi Capitals' nine-wicket loss to Royal Challengers Bengaluru, citing his old remarks on Stephen Fleming. The attack intensified scrutiny of Delhi's tactics, team calls and familiar mid-season slide after three straight defeats.

How critics made Shreyas solve his short-ball issue: Wanted to prove them wrong Shreyas Iyer said criticism over his short-ball weakness pushed him to improve as the PBKS captain opened up on fixing the issue and becoming one of IPL's best finishers.

Off target: Internet erupts over Vaibhav Sooryavanshi question to Manu Bhaker At an event in New Delhi celebrating 75 years of Indian shooting excellence, Manu Bhaker was asked about cricket prodigy Vaibhav Sooryavanshi. While Bhaker's response was a masterclass in maturity, the question itself has drawn sharp criticism, reigniting the debate over the Indian media's enduring obsession with cricket.

Wrestling Federation deliberately blocking my return: Vinesh Phogat Vinesh Phogat claimed the Wrestling Federation of India is deliberately trying to stop her comeback by blocking her National Open Ranking Tournament registration ahead of the Asian Games trials.

IPL 2026, PBKS vs RR preview: Iyer's unbeaten Punjab face Sooryavanshi storm PBKS bring an unbeaten run and record-breaking batting into a high-scoring clash vs RR. All eyes, though, remain on Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's red-hot form.

Sindhu's heartbreaking loss triggers India's Uber Cup exit, blanked 0-5 by China PV Sindhu lost to Wang Zhiyi in a thrilling three-game contest as India suffered a 0-5 defeat to China and was eliminated from the Uber Cup 2026. Sindhu squandered the win against Wang, despite leading in the decider.

The Krunal Pandya bouncer: How RCB spinner went back in time to save his future IPL 2026: Krunal Pandya's unlikely bouncer is unsettling batters in a league tilted heavily towards power-hitting. By revisiting instincts forged on matting wickets in his village, he has reinvented himself for T20 cricket's unforgiving present.

IPL Play of the Day: Saturday night's 264 hangover drowns DC on windy Monday Three days, two heartbreaking losses. Delhi Capitals have lost their way in the Indian Premier League. How did the team manage to get bowled out for just 75 runs at their own home? We discuss in the IPL Play of the Day.

Mitchell Santner ruled out of IPL 2026 as troubles pile up for Mumbai IndiansMumbai Indians have been dealt a major setback with Mitchell Santner ruled out of IPL 2026 after suffering a left shoulder injury during their match against Chennai Super Kings at the Wankhede Stadium on April 24.

How bad was Delhi Capitals' defeat against RCB? Explained in numbersIPL 2026, DC vs RCB: Rajat Patidar's Royal Challengers Bengaluru hammered the Delhi Capitals by nine wickets in what was a one-sided contest on Monday, April 27 at the Arun Jaitley Stadium in New Delhi.

India get reality check before Women's T20 World Cup as South Africa win series 4-1SAW vs INDW, 5th T20I: India suffered another batting debacle as Laura Wolvaardt's South Africa won by 23 runs in Benoni on Monday, April 27 to win the five-match series 4-1.

DC vs RCB: Hazlewood-Bhuvi storm leaves Delhi Capitals in the dustIPL 2026, DC vs RCB: A devastating new-ball blitz from Josh Hazlewood and Bhuvneshwar Kumar dismantled Delhi Capitals for just 75, before a literal dust storm and a cameo from Virat Kohli saw RCB gallop to a dominant 9-wicket victory on Monday.

Axar Patel clueless on DC's IPL slump: Even I don't understand what's happeningDelhi Capitals captain Axar Patel said that he had no clue why his team had suddenly dropped form in the Indian Premier League. DC were blown away by RCB at the Arun Jaitley Stadium.

Virat Kohli in a league of his own, completes 9000 IPL runs in styleIPL 2026, DC vs RCB: Virat Kohli became the first batter to score 9000 runs in the tournament's history when Royal Challengers Bengaluru faced Delhi Capitals on April 27 at the Arun Jaitley Stadium in New Delhi.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi turning heads in Pakistan: Does he have AI chip in his bat?Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's heroics in the Indian Premier League have turned heads in Pakistan as well. Amid the flood of reactions to the teenage sensation's fearless batting, one of Pakistan's well-known cricket YouTubers even joked about whether Vaibhav has an AI chip installed in his bat.

49 stays safe: Delhi Capitals fans celebrate as DC avoid embarrassing IPL recordIPL 2026, DC vs RCB: Delhi Capitals fans celebrated after the team avoided registering the lowest total in the tournament's history during their match against Royal Challengers Bengaluru at the Arun Jaitley Stadium in New Delhi.

Uno reverse on Rinku: Mohsin gets KKR star's bat after Lucknow heroicsAfter Kolkata Knight Riders' super over win over Lucknow Super Giants, Mohsin Khan asked Rinku Singh for one of his bats. Following some help from Rishabh Pant, the LSG pacer was able to find success.

13 for 6: Delhi Capitals record worst-ever powerplay in IPL historyDelhi Capitals recorded the worst ever powerplay in the history of the Indian Premier League. Playing against Royal Challengers Bengaluru at home, Delhi put up a horror show with the bat.

My kids call him Shreyas Bhaiya: Preity Zinta praises Iyer's warm sidePunjab Kings co-owner Preity Zinta praised Shreyas Iyer as an inspiring and strategic leader, while also revealing her children share a special bond with him, lovingly calling him Shreyas Bhaiya.

2 days after 152, KL Rahul fails in Delhi, falls prey to red-hot Josh HazlewoodKL Rahul fell for one as Delhi Capitals suffered a brutal top-order collapse against the visitors. Two days after his 152, Rahul fell to Josh Hazlewood as DC crumbled in-front of the new ball attack of RCB.

Who is Sahil Parakh? Maharashtra teenager makes IPL debut for Delhi CapitalsIPL 2026, DC vs PBKS: Delhi Capitals handed 18-year-old Sahil Parakh his IPL debut against Royal Challengers Bengaluru, bringing him in amid multiple changes following a tough defeat to Punjab Kings.

PBKS all-rounder Azmatullah Omarzai's mother dies, Afghanistan teammates confirmAzmatullah Omarzai, the Afghanistan and Punjab Kings all-rounder, has lost his mother, with his national teammates Mohammad Nabi and Karim Janat confirming the news on social media.

DC vs RCB Highlights: Bhuvneshwar, Hazlewood sink DC's ship in DelhiDelhi Capitals (DC) vs Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB) Highlights, IPL 2026: Royal Challengers Bengaluru (77/1 in 6.3 overs) chasing 76 vs DC. Devdutt Padikkal and Virat Kohli have finished the game for RCB. An exceptional barrage of shots from Padikkal and Kohli has helped RCB claim a 9-wicket victory over DC.

Best IQ award? LSG anger KKR fans with honour for Shami for Raghuvanshi wicketLucknow Super Giants gave Mohammed Shami their IQ award for the tactical moment that led to Angkrish Raghuvanshi's obstructing-the-field dismissal against Kolkata Knight Riders. The gesture reignited anger among KKR fans, while Raghuvanshi was later fined for his on-field reaction.

Mohsin Khan an incredible athlete, will play for India soon: LSG coach LangerJustin Langer hailed Mohsin Khan after the Lucknow Super Giants pacer claimed IPL 2026's first five-wicket haul against Kolkata Knight Riders. The spell highlighted his return to fitness and reinforced Langer's belief that an India call-up is not far away.

South Africa vs India, 5th Women's T20I: Highlights from BenoniSAW vs INDW, 5th T20I Live Score: India lost the fifth and final T20I by 23 runs at the Willowmoore Park in Benoni as South Africa won the five-match series 4-1.

Can I open? Chahal's cheeky PBKS question gets hilarious reply from Preity ZintaYuzvendra Chahal asked Preity Zinta on X if he could open the batting for Punjab Kings in one IPL 2026 match. However, Zinta stole the show with a witty response back to the PBKS spinner .

Thomas Cup: India storm into quarterfinal after hammering Australia 5-0Thomas Cup 2026: 2022 champions India beat Australia 5-0 in Horsens to book their Thomas Cup quarter-final place. The result kept them atop Group A and set up a table-deciding clash against China.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
